name: .NET Assembly Analyzer
description: Static triage of .NET / CLR managed assemblies (.dll/.exe) with dnfile: runtime version, assembly + referenced assemblies, type/method counts, IL-only vs mixed-mode, strong-name signing, unmanaged module refs, and the P/Invoke (DllImport) native-API surface classified by capability. Emits DOTNET.* atoms. Parses CLR metadata only — never loads or runs the assembly.

prerequisites.install_hint: Install Python 3.8+. dnfile is vendored (pure-python), so no network/install at analysis time. (No .NET runtime needed — this is static metadata parsing.)

safety.notes: Static CLR-metadata parse (dnfile). Never loads the assembly into a runtime, JITs, or executes it.

payload.note: Vendored via `uv pip install --target runtime/site` (pure-python, portable; NO .NET runtime needed). Complements pe-analyze: a native PE routes there, a managed PE is analysed here. The P/Invoke surface is where pure-IL malware touches the OS.
